title: Evotec Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson
summary: Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Evotec SE (NASDAQ: EVO) following the company's "Horizon" restructuring initiative announcement. The investigation will focus on whether Evotec's executive officers complied with federal securities laws, given the projected revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and significant restructuring charges for fiscal year 2026. Shareholders who suffered losses are encouraged to contact Johnson Fistel for more information on potentially recovering their investments.

title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Edge Lower as the IEA’s Historic Oil Release Fails to Ease Energy Prices
summary: The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) both closed lower on Wednesday despite an inflation update and a historic oil release announcement from the IEA. Rising oil prices, exacerbated by instability in the Middle East, negatively impacted the market. While the energy sector saw gains, consumer staples suffered due to increased costs and inflation fears.

title: Charles Schwab (SCHW) Finalizes Acquisition of Forge Global to Expand Private Market Access
summary: Charles Schwab (NYSE: SCHW) has completed its acquisition of Forge Global Holdings Inc., integrating Forge's private market access capabilities to allow individual investors and RIAs to invest in pre-IPO companies. This strategic move aims to democratize access to private company growth, aligning with Schwab's goal of increasing transparency and reducing costs in this sector. Forge will continue operations, gradually introducing its specialized products to Schwab's client base, with Forge shareholders receiving $45 per share.

title: TE Connectivity announces 10% increase in quarterly cash dividend to $0.78
summary: TE Connectivity plc announced a 10% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.78 per ordinary share, payable on June 12, 2026. This represents an increase from the previous $0.71 dividend. The company's board of directors also authorized a $3.0 billion increase in its share repurchase program, and shareholders reelected all 13 board members during the annual general meeting.

title: Regions Bank Taps Whitney Stewart Russell to Drive Innovation Across Consumer Products and Origination Partnerships
summary: Regions Bank has appointed Whitney Stewart Russell as head of Consumer Products and Origination Partnerships. Russell brings over 25 years of experience, including her recent role as President of Digital and Financial Solutions at Fiserv, to drive innovation and enhance customer experience in Regions' consumer banking division. Her leadership will focus on modernizing platforms, strengthening customer engagement, and accelerating growth in the bank's key markets.

title: Stocks Pressured by Higher Oil Prices, But Positive Oracle AI News Helps Tech Stocks
summary: US stock markets experienced downward pressure due to rising oil prices and increased geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, despite the IEA's decision to release strategic oil reserves. However, positive AI-related news from Oracle provided a boost to technology stocks, mitigating some of the broader market's declines. The CPI report, though near five-year lows, remains above the Fed's target, with inflation expected to worsen due to higher oil prices.

title: Northern Trust Corp. Experiences Evaluation Revision Amid Strong Market Performance Indicators
summary: Northern Trust Corp. has seen its evaluation revised due to current market dynamics, with its stock showing a slight increase and outperforming the S&P 500 significantly over the past year. Technical indicators suggest a positive outlook with bullish trends, though the Dow Theory presents a short-term bearish view. The company's resilience and competitive positioning within the capital markets sector are highlighted by its performance and volatility measures.

title: Why Kroger (KR) Is Up 5.2% After Q4 Profit Beat And Fresh Buybacks And What's Next
summary: Kroger reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results with increased profit and announced multi-billion-dollar share repurchases. While full-year net income declined slightly on flat sales, the company's focus on efficiency, digital, and private labels aims to boost profitability despite a modest 2026 identical-sales outlook. The article reviews Kroger's investment narrative, fair value estimates, and key risks associated with rising operating costs and thin profit margins.

title: A Look At Lamb Weston (LW) Valuation After Starboard Value Presses For Deeper Cost Cuts And Asset Review
summary: Starboard Value is urging Lamb Weston (LW) to implement deeper cost cuts and consider selling some Asia Pacific assets, following a period of underperformance for the stock. Despite a consensus analyst target of $63.727, Lamb Weston's current stock price of $44.48 suggests it may be undervalued, positioning it as a potential reset opportunity, though risks like cost inflation and pricing pressure remain. Investors are encouraged to review the full picture, including key rewards and warning signs, to determine if this activism-driven focus on cost discipline will lead to future value creation.

title: Form 424B2 GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
summary: GS Finance Corp. has filed a Form 424B2 for $45,500,000 Step Down Trigger Autocallable Notes due 2031, guaranteed by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. These notes are linked to the performance of the S&P 500 Index, EURO STOXX 50 Index, and the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF. Investors face significant risks, including potential loss of their entire investment and exposure to the market risk of each underlying asset without interest payments.

summary: Team, Inc. (TISI) announced its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings will be released after market close on March 12, 2026, followed by a conference call on March 13, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Central Time. The call will discuss financial and operational results and will be accessible via toll-free numbers and webcast. The article also provides details on recent insider trading activity, revenue figures, and hedge fund movements related to TISI stock.

title: Olive Garden’s Buy One, Take One offer returns
summary: Olive Garden is bringing back its popular Buy One, Take One deal for the second consecutive year, starting next week at $14.99. This offer, which returned last year after a five-year absence, allows customers to purchase a pasta entrée with unlimited soup or salad and breadsticks, and then take home a chilled second entrée for reheating. The initiative aims to combat inflation and decision fatigue, providing two meals for the price of one.

title: Danaher: $9.9 Billion Acquisition Of Masimo To Expand Diagnostics Portfolio
summary: Danaher announced its intent to acquire Masimo Corporation for $9.9 billion, paying $180 per share in cash. This acquisition aims to bolster Danaher's diagnostics portfolio with Masimo's pulse oximetry and patient monitoring technologies. The deal is expected to be accretive to Danaher's earnings and deliver substantial synergies, with closing anticipated in the second half of 2026.

title: How DuPont’s Expanded WAVE PRO Water Modeling Platform At DuPont de Nemours (DD) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: DuPont Water Solutions has expanded its WAVE PRO digital modeling platform to include reverse osmosis and nanofiltration, creating a single web-based design tool for various water treatment applications. This enhancement strengthens DuPont's data-driven approach in the water treatment sector and aligns with its strategic pivot towards high-value specialties like water and healthcare. While the WAVE PRO expansion reinforces the water thesis, near-term investment drivers still depend on post-Qnity spin execution and managing PFAS liabilities, with analysts having varied revenue and earnings expectations for DuPont by 2028.

title: Chevron To Pay $1M In Civil Penalty For Violation Of Clean Air Act
summary: Chevron has agreed to pay a civil penalty of just over $1 million and retired credits worth about $3.6 million to settle violations of the Clean Air Act's Renewable Fuel Standard program. The company invalidly generated over 2.2 million advanced biofuel production credits (RINs) from January to August 2022 on renewable diesel that had already been used for RIN generation and sold to third parties. Meanwhile, Chevron's stock (CVX) has seen increased focus and a 25% jump in 2026 due to rising oil supply pressures related to the conflict in the Middle East.

title: Why Conagra (CAG) Stock Is Falling Today
summary: Conagra Brands (CAG) stock dropped over 5% after its competitor, The Campbell's Co (CPB), reported disappointing quarterly results and lowered its full-year profit outlook due to weak demand. This news led to a broad sector selloff, impacting investor confidence in similar companies like Conagra. Additionally, an increase in bearish options trading on Conagra suggested investor nervousness ahead of its own upcoming earnings announcement.

title: Citigroup (C) Issues First Digitally Native Structured Note via Euroclear
summary: Citigroup (C) has successfully issued its first digitally native structured note via Euroclear's D-FMI platform, marking a significant step in the wealth management industry. This transaction, issued under English Law, leverages Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) to enhance efficiency and transparency while maintaining traditional investment profiles. The initiative, involving multiple internal divisions, signifies a move towards embedding digital innovation into core financial ecosystems.

title: Boeing falls on fresh wiring flaw found on 737 Max planes
summary: Boeing's stock fell due to a newly discovered wiring flaw in its 737 Max planes. This issue could potentially lead to a short circuit, causing an uncommanded nose-down dive. The company is evaluating whether a redesign is necessary.

title: 12 Best Stocks to Buy According to Billionaire Mario Gabelli
summary: This article, part of a larger list, highlights Ametek Inc. (NYSE: AME) as one of Mario Gabelli's top stock picks. It details GAMCO Investors' significant long-term holding in Ametek, the impressive returns generated, and the company's strong financial outlook for 2026, including expected sales and EPS growth, and a recent dividend hike.

The company is now exploring its next steps, including a potential sale, after previously cutting 50% of its workforce and eliminating R&D.

title: Dynex Capital Keeps Monthly Dividend at $0.17 a Share, Payable April 1 to Holders of Record March 23
summary: Dynex Capital, Inc. has announced that it will maintain its monthly dividend at $0.17 per share. This dividend is payable on April 1 to shareholders of record as of March 23. The company, a mortgage real estate investment trust (REIT), aims to provide attractive risk-adjusted returns to its shareholders through regular dividends and capital appreciation of its investments.

title: Leavenworth clears way for ICE detention center reopening
summary: The Leavenworth City Commission has approved a special-use permit for CoreCivic to reopen its Midwest Regional Reception Center, formerly a prison, as an ICE detention facility. The 4-1 vote allows the center to accept immigration detainees within weeks after about a year of legal disputes. Despite public opposition citing harm to immigrant families, supporters highlighted the jobs provided, with the city noting it retains the power to revoke the permit if conditions are not met.

title: A Look At Lamb Weston (LW) Valuation After Starboard Value Presses For Deeper Cost Cuts And Asset Review
summary: Activist investor Starboard Value is pushing Lamb Weston (LW) to implement deeper cost cuts, specifically in SG&A, and to consider selling certain Asia Pacific assets. This pressure comes after a period of poor stock performance for Lamb Weston, with a significant decline in share price and total shareholder return. Despite the stock trading below analyst targets and an intrinsic value estimate, suggesting it might be undervalued, the company faces scrutiny as it exits the S&P 500.

title: How DuPont’s Expanded WAVE PRO Water Modeling Platform At DuPont de Nemours (DD) Has Changed Its Investment Story

This enhancement strengthens DuPont's position in data-driven water treatment design and supports its strategic pivot towards higher-value specialties like water and healthcare. While the expansion reinforces the long-term water thesis, the company's immediate investment narrative still faces uncertainties related to legal liabilities and margin stabilization, alongside ongoing efforts to balance technology investments with shareholder returns.

title: International Tower Hill Mines Files 2025 Year End Financial Results
summary: International Tower Hill Mines Ltd. announced that it has filed its audited Year-End Financial Statements and associated Management Discussion and Analysis on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025. These documents are available on SEDAR+, EDGAR, and the company's website, with hard copies also available to shareholders upon request. The company holds a 100% interest in the Livengood Gold Project in Alaska.

title: Pizza Hut updated its Hand-Tossed Pizza recipe for the first time in a decade
summary: Pizza Hut has updated its Hand-Tossed Pizza recipe for the first time in over a decade, introducing a new crust with a lighter, airier bite seasoned with a Garlic-Parm Hut Blend. This revamp is part of their new "Hut Crust" marketing platform, which includes a $10 large three-topping pizza deal and a national ad campaign. The company is also running a contest to find a "Crust Connoisseur" offering a $31,415.92 payment and free pizza for a year.

title: Con Edison Executives to Meet with Investors in March 2026
summary: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (Con Edison) executives will meet with investors the week of March 16, 2026. The company operates as a holding company providing various energy-related products and services through its subsidiaries, including Consolidated Edison Company of New York, Inc., Orange and Rockland Utilities, Inc., and Con Edison Transmission, Inc.

summary: The Rosen Law Firm has filed a class action lawsuit against Trip.com Group Limited (NASDAQ: TCOM) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between April 30, 2024, and January 13, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Trip.com made false and misleading statements about its regulatory risks concerning monopolistic business activities. Investors who wish to serve as lead plaintiff must do so by May 11, 2026.

title: Argentina Transportadora de Gas del Sur anuncia inversión por US$ 3.000 millones en proyecto de líquidos de gas natural
summary: Argentina's Transportadora de Gas del Sur (TGS) announced a $3 billion investment in a natural gas liquids project in the Vaca Muerta formation. The project, which includes a processing plant, a polyduct, and fractionation and storage plants near Buenos Aires, is expected to generate $1.2 billion in annual exports once operational in 45 months. This development is crucial for expanding Vaca Muerta's production and is being presented under Argentina's large investment stimulus regime.

title: Regions Bank Taps Whitney Stewart Russell to Drive Innovation Across Consumer Products and Origination Partnerships

Russell brings over 25 years of experience in digital banking, financial accounting, and lending platforms, and will focus on modernizing deposit and lending platforms and developing new payment options. This strategic hire aims to strengthen customer relationships and further distinguish Regions Bank in the evolving banking landscape.

title: U.S. Bancorp's new CEO realized $8.6 million in 2025
summary: Gunjan Kedia, the new CEO of U.S. Bancorp, realized $8.6 million in total compensation for the year ending December 31, 2025. Her compensation included a $1.2 million annual salary, a cash bonus of over $3.2 million, and long-term equity awards. Kedia, who took over as CEO in April 2025, also led the company's acquisition of investment banking firm BTIG for approximately $1 billion.

title: Fair Isaac Stock Tumbles. The Credit Bureaus Swing Big With Price Cuts.
summary: Fair Isaac's stock has been falling for three consecutive days due to an ongoing pricing war with the three major credit bureaus. The company is actively working to defend its market share in the face of these competitive pressures.

This ship emphasizes multigenerational family travel with attractions like the Aqua Slidecoaster, an outdoor amusement park area, and a multi-level obstacle course. The Norwegian Luna will offer various Caribbean and Bermuda itineraries from Miami and New York City, demonstrating the company's commitment to innovative design and family-friendly cruise experiences.

title: Kroger answers questions about its plans for a new grocery store in Keller
summary: Kroger held a public meeting in Keller to address community concerns regarding its proposed new Marketplace grocery store near Keller Town Center. The new store will be a scaled-down version of a previous plan from 2015 and aims to mitigate traffic congestion. Residents, however, still express concerns about traffic, noise, property values, and the fate of the old Kroger building.

title: The Mosaic Company (MOS) Climbs 10% on Rare Earth Expansion

Its subsidiary, Mosaic Fertilizantes P&K Limitada, in partnership with Rainbow Rare Earths Ltd., completed an economic assessment for a new mining site in Minas Gerais, indicating significant potential for rare earth element production. The project aims for processing facility construction next year and official production by 2030, aligning with Mosaic's strategy to unlock additional value from existing assets.

title: Tech executive and MIT educator to leave Centerspace board

Management expressed gratitude for her leadership, innovation, and extensive technology and cybersecurity expertise. summary: Healthcare Triangle, Inc. announced a Board-approved share repurchase plan of up to $2 million, effective immediately as of March 9, 2026. This discretionary program aims to enhance shareholder value through open-market or negotiated transactions, reflecting confidence in the company's long-term strategy. The plan allows management flexibility, but does not obligate the company to buy back any specific amount of shares and can be suspended or discontinued.

title: Biotech Longeveron secures $15M, cash runway past 2026 HLHS trial readout
summary: Longeveron (NASDAQ: LGVN) has successfully closed an initial $15 million tranche of a private placement, with potential for another $15 million linked to milestones from its Phase 2b ELPIS II HLHS trial. This financing, led by Coastlands Capital, is expected to extend the company's cash runway into Q4 2026, past the anticipated Q3 2026 topline data readout. Investors also received rights to 50% of net proceeds from any future Rare Pediatric Disease Priority Review Voucher (PRV) for its HLHS program.

title: Oil-Dri Corp of America Q2: Revenue $238.2M, EPS $1.93
summary: Oil-Dri Corp of America reported consolidated net sales of $238.2 million for the six-month period, a 3% year-over-year decrease, primarily due to softer volumes. Diluted net income per common share was $1.93, down from $2.01 a year earlier, with gross profit and income from operations also declining. The company cited factors like reduced B2B fluid and animal health volumes, operational disruptions from Winter Storm Fern, and noted growth in agricultural carriers and co-packaged cat litter.

title: Homeowner blames OGE wiring goof that damaged his appliances

OGE is investigating the incident, which occurred last December, and has stated its intention to work with the customer towards a full resolution. The homeowner reported flashing lights and an electric shock incident following the contractor's mistake.

The company is accelerating the development of PLN-101095 with a Phase 1b indication expansion trial set to begin enrollment in Q2 2026, with interim data expected in 2027. Financially, Pliant reported a reduced net loss of $23.6 million in Q4 2025 and has $192.4 million in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments, sufficient to fund operations into the second half of 2028.

title: Magnite partners with NOVA Entertainment for programmatic audio ads

This collaboration allows advertisers to programmatically access the network's digital audio inventory via Magnite's platform, enabling them to connect with shoppers directly at retail locations. The partnership addresses a growing demand for automated audio buying, with 72% of Australian audio buyers purchasing programmatically in 2025.

summary: Strattec Security Corporation (Nasdaq: STRT) announced that its President & CEO, Jennifer Slater, and CFO, Matthew Pauli, will present and host investor meetings at the Sidoti Small Cap Conference on March 18, 2026. title: Divergent climate policies to drive a chaotic transition
summary: The article analyzes the divergent climate policy paths taken by the European Union and the United States in February 2026, leading to a potentially chaotic energy transition. While the EU has strengthened its decarbonization goals with a legally binding 2040 GHG reduction target, the U.S. has moved to dismantle federal GHG regulations. Both regions face the challenge of balancing sustainability, energy security, and affordability, which is expected to accelerate investment in energy efficiency and renewables, albeit at different paces and with varying complexities.

title: How to watch Starbucks' 2026 virtual shareholder meeting

title: President Trump touts TrumpRx, pharmaceutical manufacturing push in Cincinnati visit

Trump also addressed the upcoming midterms, criticizing Democrats and Kentucky Rep. Thomas Massie, while discussing the nation's economy and foreign policy regarding Iran.

title: Insulet Presents Promising Study Results for Fully Closed-Loop Automated Insulin Delivery System for Adults with Type 2 Diabetes

The study showed a significant improvement in time in range (TIR) to 68% with no boluses, a 24% increase over standard therapy, and very low time below range. This innovation aims to simplify diabetes management for adults with Type 2 Diabetes by automating insulin delivery without requiring mealtime interactions, with a pivotal study planned for 2026 and a commercial launch projected for 2028.

The integration allows teams to use Keysight instruments directly within Sceptre, reducing friction and enabling more adaptable signal workflows, especially for operations that rely on multi-vendor equipment.

title: Chubb picked to lead US insurance backstop for Hormuz shipping
summary: Chubb has been selected as the lead US insurer for a government-backed program designed to provide an insurance backstop for commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative, run by the US International Development Finance Corp. (DFC), aims to mitigate war-related risks and facilitate crude and refined product flows, with DFC reinsuring up to $20 billion in losses and Chubb issuing primary policies to shipowners. Despite the financial protection, concerns remain about the physical security of seafarers in the conflict zone.

title: Chubb picked to lead US insurance backstop for Hormuz shipping

title: Biogen builds case for Spinraza successor with fresh data

Salanersen, positioned as a successor to Biogen's Spinraza, achieved a 75% reduction in neurofilament light chain levels and enabled half of the trial participants to reach a motor function milestone. Biogen also outlined plans for a late-stage study involving three trials for various patient groups, aiming to secure salanersen's role in the future SMA treatment landscape.

title: Medical device giant Stryker confirms cyberattack as employees say devices were wiped
summary: Medical device manufacturer Stryker has confirmed a cyberattack that disrupted its operations, with employees reporting devices were wiped. Handala, known for deploying wiper malware and previously tied to Iran-based threat actors, has a history of targeting Israeli entities.

title: The CAT’s out of the Bag: No Estimation Allowed for Tax Refunds in Ohio
summary: The Ohio Supreme Court recently ruled on a significant Commercial Activity Tax (CAT) dispute, clarifying that while the ultimate destination of goods is key for sourcing, taxpayers cannot rely on estimates for refund claims. In Jones Apparel Group/Nine West Holdings v. Harris, the court acknowledged that post-sale evidence is permissible but ultimately denied a refund due to insufficient documentary evidence, emphasizing the need for specific, verifiable, and period-accurate data to challenge Ohio sourcing determinations and quantify tax reductions.

title: Quartzsea Acquisition Amends IPO Underwriting Terms; Deferred Fee Set at 4% With Redemption Cap
summary: Quartzsea Acquisition (QSEA) has amended its IPO underwriting agreement to revise the calculation and payment of the deferred underwriting commission. The commission is now set at 4.00% of gross proceeds, payable from the trust upon the initial business combination's closing, and capped at 4.00% of funds remaining after redemptions. This amendment, signed with Polaris Advisory Partners and Kingswood Capital Partners, also allows underwriters to waive the deferred commission before the business combination to better align fees with post-redemption trust balances.

title: Sensible Portions Says No Artificial Flavors or Preservatives. A Class Action Lawsuit Says That Claim is False.
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Hain Celestial Group Inc., maker of Sensible Portions snacks, alleging their "No Artificial Flavors or Preservatives" claim is false. The suit contends that citric acid, an ingredient in their Veggie Puffs, Veggie Straws, and Apple Straws, is a synthetic chemical preservative. The plaintiff, Samantha Jones, seeks compensatory damages, restitution, and injunctive relief, arguing that the company misleads consumers about the naturalness and preservative-free nature of its products.

Gray states that it is prepared to finalize an agreement without this contentious provision and will pursue legal action for damages incurred due to Dish's conduct.

title: TYTL Closes Strategic Investment from Strobe and Fifth Era; Launches Blockchain-Based Fractional Real Estate Equity Platform with Beeline and Anchorage Digital Bank Partnerships
summary: TYTL Corp, a residential real estate tokenization platform, has secured seed funding from Strobe Ventures and Fifth Era. This platform offers homeowners a debt-free alternative to access home equity by acquiring fractional equity interests in prime residential properties, with the first 11 acquisitions already completed.

title: CEO Warns Of Next Oil Supply Shock - United States Oil Fund (ARCA:USO)
summary: Robert Price, CEO of March GL and incoming CEO of Greenland Energy Company, warns that markets are underestimating the structural risks to global oil supply, despite recent geopolitical events. He emphasizes the declining investment in long-term supply and the vulnerability of Western supply chains to geopolitical chokepoints. Price argues that new conventional resources, such as those in Greenland's Jameson Land Basin, are crucial for future energy security as current capacity declines and short-cycle shale projects prove insufficient for long-term needs.

title: Marcus & Millichap Releases 2026 Milwaukee Retail Investment Forecast Report

Key findings include projected 4% household income growth, a modest rise in retail vacancy to 4.1%, and a moderation in new construction after a 2025 high. Despite a slight softening in average asking rents, the market is positioned for steady performance due to limited new supply and healthy consumer fundamentals.

title: SmartVMI transforms inventory operations with active stock management, DXP Enterprises asserts

SmartVMI leverages on-site assessments and dynamically manages stock levels based on real demand, including slow-moving items. This initiative aligns with DXP's broader strategy of integrating technology with client needs, focusing on operational outcomes, and strengthening partnerships.
